title: Rich Vietnamese Nectarine Braised Porridge
description: A hearty, comforting porridge featuring briny nectarines and a hint of yuzu. Perfect for a hangover cure or a relaxing breakfast.

ingredients.name: oil
ingredients.notes: neutral oil like vegetable or canola

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: use 2 cups for a thicker porridge

instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and ginger and saut7e until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the halved nectarines and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to soften.
Add the yuzu juice, fish sauce, and sugar. Stir to combine.
Add the rice and water to the saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 18-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir the porridge occasionally to prevent it from sticking to the bottom of the saucepan.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Use a potato masher or the back of a spoon to mash the nectarines and break up any clumps.
Season the porridge with salt to taste.
Serve the porridge hot, garnished with additional yuzu juice and nectarine slices if desired.
Note: Yuzu juice is a citrus juice commonly used in Vietnamese cuisine. It can be found in some Asian markets or online.
